title: centaurium umbellatum subsp. capitatum
intro: Centaurium umbellatum subsp. capitatum, commonly known as the white mountain centaury, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the gentian family. This plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which contain iridoid glycosides that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Centaurium umbellatum subsp. capitatum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a potential remedy for various ailments. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an herbal tea or tincture, with the roots and leaves being used in infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
